commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a manual `QA Lab Smoke Profile` workflow that calls `qa-profile-evidence.yml` with `smoke-ci`, makes the callable `OPENAI_API_KEY` secret optional, and updates the workflow guard test.

PR surface: Tests +3, Config +32. Total +35 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the merge-readiness concern by source inspection: the wrapper delegates to the shared QA profile workflow and the PR body provides local/static checks rather than a successful dispatch run. This is not a current-main product bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review is needed because the remaining blockers are workflow ownership, optional-secret contract acceptance, and default-branch dispatch proof, not a narrow mechanical repair.

target.title: ci: add manual QA Lab smoke profile evidence
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

PR #95880 moved QA profile evidence generation into the shared `qa-profile-evidence.yml` workflow, and PR #95971 tightened that workflow so QA profile failures always fail after evidence upload. This PR adds only the manual smoke-profile caller that requests `smoke-ci` evidence from the shared workflow, without duplicating checkout, build, QA execution, evidence validation, or artifact upload logic.

## Why This Change Was Made

`smoke-ci` uses mock-provider mode, so the smoke caller should not pass live provider secrets. The reusable workflow now keeps `OPENAI_API_KEY` optional for callable users while existing live/profile callers can still pass it explicitly. The smoke wrapper is manual-only because the reusable workflow validates trusted refs before entering its QA job; running it on `pull_request` with trusted base refs would not test PR code.

## User Impact

No product runtime behavior changes. Maintainers get a `QA Lab Smoke Profile` workflow that can produce `smoke-ci` QA evidence and the shared `qa-profile-evidence-*` artifact/manifest when manually dispatched against a trusted branch, tag, or SHA.

## Evidence

Current PR head: `4e288d885a4`.

Recent related RomneyDa PRs checked live:

- #95971 `ci: fail QA profile evidence on QA failures`: changed the callable contract by removing `fail_on_qa_failure` and making QA failures always fail after upload.
- #95947 `feat(qa): add "all" taxonomy profile`: adds profile flexibility but does not require a smoke wrapper change.
- #95944 `perf(qa-lab): speed up unified QA suites`: QA suite/runtime performance changes; no wrapper contract change.
- #95898 `ci: simplify maturity scorecard QA evidence inputs`: keeps the maturity scorecard caller on the shared workflow pattern.
- #95920 `feat(qa): wire Crabline fake provider env`: open, transport/runtime work; no current workflow wrapper change needed.

Local checks:

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml .github/workflows/qa-profile-evidence.yml .github/workflows/maturity-scorecard.yml`
- `uvx zizmor==1.22.0 --config .github/zizmor.yml --persona=regular --min-severity=medium --min-confidence=medium .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml .github/workflows/qa-profile-evidence.yml .github/workflows/maturity-scorecard.yml`
- `python3 scripts/check-composite-action-input-interpolation.py`
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml .github/workflows/qa-profile-evidence.yml .github/workflows/maturity-scorecard.yml && uvx zizmor==1.22.0 --config .github/zizmor.yml --persona=regular --min-severity=medium --min-confidence=medium .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml .github/workflows/qa-profile-evidence.yml .github/workflows/maturity-scorecard.yml && python3 scripts/check-composite-action-input-interpolation.py && node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts"`

Proof limitations:

- `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs` remains blocked locally because `/usr/bin/python3` is Python 3.9.6 and the pinned `zizmor==1.22.0` pre-commit hook requires Python 3.10+. The same pinned `zizmor` audit passes directly through `uvx`.
- Manual `workflow_dispatch` cannot be exercised from this PR branch until the workflow exists on the default branch. The validated surface here is the workflow contract, syntax/security linting, and the reusable workflow caller shape.

## Linked Context

Related #94276
Related #94283
Related #94306
Related #95880
Related #95971

## Risk Checklist

- User-visible behavior: no product runtime behavior changed.
- Config/environment/migration behavior: no runtime config or migration behavior changed.
- Security/auth/secrets/tool execution: narrowly changed through workflow wiring. The smoke wrapper passes no live provider secret, and the reusable workflow secret is optional for mock-profile callers.
- Highest-risk area: callable workflow secret semantics.
- Mitigation: existing live/profile callers can still pass `OPENAI_API_KEY`; the guard test now asserts the reusable secret remains optional so mock-backed callers are not forced to provide live credentials.
target.author: solvely-colin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
